iteration_notes: Corrected Buddy Carter and Mike Collins voting records by replacing the Senate Laken Riley Act citation with the House roll call for H.R. 29 / H.R. 7511, which House members actually voted on.
Removed the non-candidate 'Unitemized donations' entry from Buddy Carter's top_donors list and kept only identifiable donor entities with source URLs.

Rewrote Derek Dooley's Economy stance to be more specific and neutral, tying it to his Georgia First Contract and public emphasis on Georgia-first governance, term limits, stock-trading bans, shutdown pay, and accountability.
Verified and retained current polling and candidate summaries, while preserving neutral language and source-grounded issue stances.
Ensured all voting_record entries use bill_name and vote fields with allowed vote values only, and kept source objects attached to donor entries.
title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Georgia
description: Georgia’s 2026 U.S. Senate race is a closely watched contest in a state that has been closely divided in recent federal elections. Incumbent Democrat Jon Ossoff is seeking reelection, while Republicans are competing in a primary that includes Reps. Buddy Carter and Mike Collins and former football coach Derek Dooley. Early polling shows Ossoff ahead of each potential Republican general-election opponent, but the GOP primary remains unsettled. The race is likely to turn on turnout, candidate quality, fundraising, and whether Republicans can unify behind a nominee who can compete statewide.

candidates.name: Jon Ossoff
candidates.summary: Jon Ossoff is the incumbent Democratic U.S. senator from Georgia and is running for reelection in 2026. His public messaging has emphasized health care, infrastructure, public safety, and oversight of federal agencies, while also framing the race around national partisan control of the Senate.

Healthcare.stance: Ossoff says he is focused on lowering health care and prescription drug costs, protecting access to care, and oversight of health-related federal systems.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Launches Inquiry into Impacts of Staffing Shortages and Leadership Deficiencies at Charlie Norwood VA Medical Center
Sen. Ossoff Helps Pass Bipartisan Bill into Law to Lower Drug Costs for Georgians

Economy.stance: Ossoff says he is focused on lowering housing costs, protecting infrastructure investments, creating Georgia jobs, and opposing trade wars that he says raise prices and hurt small businesses.

sources.title: Five-Year Report – U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff

Climate/Energy.stance: Continues to support clean-energy investment and domestic solar manufacturing; his office says he championed legislation to supercharge American solar manufacturing, strengthen energy independence, and accelerate the transition to renewable energy.

sources.title: BREAKING: Sen. Ossoff’s Landmark Legislation to Supercharge American Solar Manufacturing Signed into Law

Reproductive Rights.stance: Ossoff opposes abortion restrictions and supports access to contraception and reproductive health care.

sources.title: Press Releases – U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff

Immigration.stance: Since late March 2026, Ossoff has continued emphasizing oversight of immigration detention conditions, including launching an inquiry into alleged abuse at the 'Alligator Alcatraz' ICE detention site and urging DHS/ICE to stop the reported use of a cage-like confinement area if confirmed. This reinforces a position combining border/security concerns with strong detainee-rights and anti-abuse oversight.

sources.title: Sens. Ossoff, Durbin Launch Investigation into Abuse Allegations at “Alligator Alcatraz”

Guns & Safety.stance: Ossoff supports gun-safety measures and frames the issue around public safety.

sources.title: Five-Year Report – U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff

Foreign Policy.stance: On March 18, 2026, Ossoff pressed the Director of National Intelligence over conflicting claims about Iran’s nuclear threat, questioning whether the intelligence community had actually assessed an 'imminent nuclear threat' and criticizing evasive answers. This shows continued focus on intelligence oversight and skepticism toward administration claims about Iran.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Presses Director of National Intelligence on Imminent Threat Posed by Iran, Fulton County Raid

Social Justice.stance: Continues to emphasize civil and human rights, including oversight of immigration detention abuses and investigations into reported abuses.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Investigation Uncovers Over 1000 Credible Reports of Human Rights Abuses in Immigration Detention
[PDF] THE ABUSE OF PREGNANT WOMEN & CHILDREN IN U.S. IMMIGRATION DETENTION

Education.stance: Ossoff says he supports education-related workforce development and strengthening education for Georgia’s next generation, including support for Georgia HBCUs.

sources.title: Five-Year Report – U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff

Tech & AI.stance: Has continued pressing for oversight of government AI use; in February 2026 he joined a Senate inquiry demanding answers about the Department of Defense’s planned use of the xAI chatbot Grok, citing concerns about antisemitic and dangerous content.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff, Colleagues Launch Inquiry into Department of Defense’s Use of AI Software Known for Antisemitic Content

Election Reform.stance: Ossoff continues to emphasize voting rights and election administration, including support for election-worker protections and broader access to voting.

sources.title: Georgia – U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff
U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff

Local Issues.stance: Continues emphasizing Georgia-specific service and infrastructure work, including public safety in Carrollton, affordable housing in Baldwin County, Georgia Ports upgrades, water infrastructure planning through the Chattahoochee River Act, and support for families experiencing homelessness.

sources.title: Georgia – U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff

candidates.name: Buddy Carter
candidates.summary: Buddy Carter is a Republican U.S. representative from coastal Georgia who entered the 2026 Senate race to challenge Ossoff. His campaign and House record present him as a conservative focused on immigration enforcement, tax and regulatory policy, and support for domestic energy production.

Healthcare.stance: Carter says he supports improving maternal health outcomes and reducing health care costs through market-oriented, innovation-focused policy; in January 2026 he promoted his Preventing Maternal Deaths Reauthorization Act, which reauthorizes state maternal mortality review committees and directs HHS to share best practices on maternal mortality prevention.

sources.title: Carter bill preventing maternal deaths and improving health outcomes for mothers heads to Senate
Chairman Carter Delivers Opening Statement at Subcommittee on Health Hearing on the Department of Health and Human Services Fiscal Year 2026 Budget

Economy.stance: Carter says his top priorities are improving the economy, getting Georgians back to work, reducing burdensome regulation, and supporting small businesses and job creators.

sources.title: Jobs and the Economy : U.S. Representative Buddy Carter

Climate/Energy.stance: Carter supports domestic energy production, nuclear power, and permitting reform, and he has criticized climate and emissions rules he views as restrictive.

sources.title: Energy : U.S. Representative Buddy Carter
Environment : U.S. Representative Buddy Carter

Reproductive Rights.stance: Carter's reviewed public materials do not provide a detailed standalone reproductive-rights platform; his broader public record aligns with Republican opposition to abortion expansion.

sources.title: Latest News : U.S. Representative Buddy Carter
Biographical Directory of the United States Congress: Buddy Carter

Immigration.stance: On February 9, 2026, Carter introduced the No Sanctuary Cities Act, which would require state and local governments to cooperate with federal immigration authorities, share custody/release information, hold certain detainees up to 48 hours, and penalize jurisdictions that limit cooperation. On March 18, 2026, he also backed the Deporting Fraudsters Act, saying illegal aliens who defraud the government or unlawfully receive benefits should be removed. His position remains strongly enforcement-first.

sources.title: Carter to force "sanctuary" cities to comply with ICE in new bill
Carter, Taylor celebrate House Passage of bill to deport aliens responsible for defrauding American taxpayers

candidates.name: Derek Dooley
candidates.summary: Derek Dooley is a former University of Tennessee football coach and Republican candidate for U.S. Senate in Georgia. His campaign has emphasized outsider status and a Georgia-first message, while reporting has also noted his limited prior political experience and voting history.

summary_sources.title: Derek Dooley didn’t vote for years. Now he wants to be a Georgia senator.
UGA School of Law Advocate 2010 Alumni

Economy.stance: Dooley says his economic approach is part of his Georgia First Contract: he emphasizes putting Georgia first, limiting his own service to two Senate terms, banning members of Congress from trading stocks or digital assets, refusing pay during a government shutdown, and holding town halls across the state to stay accountable to voters.
